calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-10-2020 Continued To Dec-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Dwight Murphy (Adminstrator) appearing in person in pro per.

The Court addressed Mr. Murphy stating that the letters must be signed and then issued by the clerk. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Accounting is continued to December 21, 2020 in Dept. 204. at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Murphy is to give notice to the creditors. Mr. Murphy stated that he tried reaching the creditor(s) twice, that they said that they did not have any additional information for him, and that he was told that the car would be collected from his Mother's Husband so that it could be sold. The Court addressed Mr. Murphy stating that if a creditor has a claim that notice needs to be given in writing and noted that there are public entities that require notice. Ms. Hilton referenced Probate Code 9202. Mr. Murphy was directed to go directly to the clerk's office after today's hearing to have the letters issued.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Aug-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Continued To May-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Continued To Apr-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dwight Murphy in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the letters of administration have not been issued, informed Mr. Murphy that he has no authority to act on behalf of the estate until the letters are issued and that the time for filing of the inventory and appraisal and for the creditor's claims to start tolling is determined by the date the letters are issued. The Court Clerk provided Mr. Murphy with the name of the appointed Probate Referee as reflected in the order appointing the administrator. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to April 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dwight Murphy in Propria Persona.

The Court explained the roll of the Administrator, explained what needs to be done to administer the estate and informed Mr. Murphy that he can not sell property without Court authorization. The Court also stated that there is no such thing as a waiver in Probate and that it should be a disclaimer. Mr. Murphy stated that he does not know where Charles Scott is and that he has a copy of the inter spousal transfer deed. The Court directed Mr. Murphy to file a declaration indicating what efforts he has made to locate Mr. Scott and to file the transfer deed. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. If Mr. Murphy discovers any assets, he is to file a petition to post bond. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is for February 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for October 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-19-2018 Continued To Oct-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
